_comment: Example AIP Credential Token — AIP spec §4.2.3 and §7.3. The token is a compact JWT (header.payload.signature). The decoded form is shown here for readability. In production, agents present the compact JWT string to relying parties. Signatures are placeholders.

aip_chain: <compact JWT for Principal Token at delegation_depth=0 — see delegation-chain.json for decoded form>

_notes.ttl: exp - iat = 3600s — standard scope TTL cap (email.*, calendar.*). Sensitive scopes (transactions.*, communicate.*, filesystem.execute) are capped at 300s.
_notes.aip_chain: The aip_chain array contains compact JWT strings. Each JWT encodes one Principal Token per principal-token.schema.json. Index 0 is always the root delegation from the human principal.
_notes.iss_equals_sub: iss equals sub — this is a non-delegated token (direct principal → agent, no sub-agent chain). Validation Step 8 Post-Check B enforces this for single-element chains.
_notes.validation: Relying parties validate this token using the 12-step algorithm in AIP spec §7.3. Steps 1-9 are registry-verifiable; Step 10 (DPoP) and Step 11 (mTLS) are relying-party infrastructure.
